schemaVersion.description: Version of the output JSON schema format

nextcladeAlgoVersion.description: Nextclade algorithm library version that produced this output

nextcladeWebVersion.description: Nextclade Web version, if output was produced via the web application

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the output was generated

cladeNodeAttrKeys.description: Descriptions of clade-like node attributes defined in the reference tree

phenotypeAttrKeys.description: Descriptions of phenotype score attributes defined in the dataset

refNodes.description: Reference node search criteria defined in the dataset

results.description: Per-sequence analysis results for sequences that were processed

errors.description: Per-sequence error records for sequences that failed processing

CladeNodeAttrKeyDesc.description: Description of a clade-like node attribute

name.description: Machine-readable identifier, must match the key used in node_attrs on tree nodes

displayName.description: Human-readable label displayed in the UI

hideInWeb.description: Exclude this attribute's column from Nextclade Web results table

skipAsReference.description: Exclude this attribute from clade founder node search and relative mutation calculation

name.description: Machine-readable identifier for this phenotype attribute.

nameFriendly.description: Human-readable display name shown in the UI.

description.description: Free-text description of this phenotype attribute.

AuspiceRefNodesDesc.description: Describes search criteria in the reference tree nodes. This is used for "Relative to" feature where you can switch mutation calling source node between "reference", "parent", "clade founder" and custom nodes described using this format.

default.description: Name of the default reference node to display. One of the `search[].name` values or a built-in: "__root__", "__parent__", "__clade_founder__".

search.description: Custom reference node search descriptions, each corresponding to an entry in the "Relative to" dropdown

builtins.description: Display name and description overrides for built-in reference node types (root, parent, clade founder)

AuspiceRefNodeSearchDesc.description: Describes criteria for selecting a reference node for the "Relative to" feature.

name.description: Unique identifier for this search entry

displayName.description: Human-readable label for the UI dropdown

description.description: Tooltip text describing when this reference node applies

criteria.description: Match criteria (OR logic between elements). Each criterion pairs query conditions with node conditions.

AuspiceRefNodeSearchCriteria.description: Search criteria pairing query sequence conditions with reference node conditions.

qry.description: Conditions the query sequence must satisfy for this criterion to apply

node.description: Conditions and search algorithm for finding the matching reference node

AuspiceNodeCriterion.description: Conditions for matching a tree node by name, clade, or clade-like attributes.

name.description: Node names to match (at least one must match)

clade.description: Clade values to match (at least one must match)

cladeNodeAttrs.description: Clade-like attribute values to match, keyed by attribute name. Each key requires at least one value match.

AuspiceRefNodeCriterion.description: Criterion for matching a reference tree node, with a specified search algorithm.

searchAlgo.description: Search algorithm to use when traversing the tree

name.description: Node names to match (at least one must match)

clade.description: Clade values to match (at least one must match)

cladeNodeAttrs.description: Clade-like attribute values to match, keyed by attribute name. Each key requires at least one value match.

AuspiceNodeSearchAlgo.description: Algorithm for searching reference tree nodes in the "Relative to" feature.

oneOf.description: Traverse from the query node toward the root, return the first (nearest) match
Traverse from the query node toward the root, return the last (earliest/deepest) match
Linear search over all tree nodes until the first match

AuspiceRefNodeBuiltinsConfig.description: Display name and description overrides for all built-in reference node types.

__root__.description: Config for the root reference node (reference sequence)

__parent__.description: Config for the parent (nearest) reference node

__clade_founder__.description: Config for the clade founder reference node

AuspiceRefNodeBuiltinConfig.description: Display name and description overrides for a built-in reference node type.

displayName.description: Override display name for this built-in reference node type

description.description: Override description for this built-in reference node type

NextcladeOutputs.title: ResultJson
NextcladeOutputs.description: Single element in `.results` array in nextclade.json file, produced by `nextclade run --output-json`. This corresponds to a single sequence in the inputs.

index.description: Zero-based position of this sequence in the input

seqName.description: Full sequence name from the FASTA header

seqId.description: Sequence identifier, first whitespace-delimited token of the FASTA header

seqDesc.description: Description portion of the FASTA header after the identifier

lenUnaligned.description: Length of the original query sequence before alignment

lenAligned.description: Length of the query sequence after alignment, in alignment coordinates

lenStripped.description: Length of the query sequence after stripping insertions relative to the reference

refName.description: Name of the reference sequence used for alignment

datasetName.description: Name of the dataset used for analysis

substitutions.description: Nucleotide substitutions relative to the reference

deletions.description: Nucleotide deletion ranges relative to the reference

totalDeletions.description: Total count of deleted nucleotide positions

insertions.description: Nucleotide insertions stripped from the alignment

totalInsertions.description: Total count of inserted nucleotide positions

missing.description: Ranges of missing (N) nucleotides in the aligned sequence

nonACGTNs.description: Ranges of ambiguous nucleotides (not A, C, G, T, or N)

nucleotideComposition.description: Per-character nucleotide counts in the aligned sequence

frameShifts.description: Frame-shifting insertions or deletions detected in CDS regions

totalFrameShifts.description: Total count of detected frame shifts

aaSubstitutions.description: Amino acid substitutions relative to reference peptides

totalAminoacidSubstitutions.description: Total count of amino acid substitutions

aaDeletions.description: Amino acid deletions relative to reference peptides

totalAminoacidDeletions.description: Total count of amino acid deletions

aaInsertions.description: Amino acid insertions relative to reference peptides

totalAminoacidInsertions.description: Total count of amino acid insertions

unknownAaRanges.description: Ranges of unknown (X) amino acids per CDS

totalUnknownAa.description: Total count of unknown (X) amino acids

aaChangesGroups.description: Groups of adjacent amino acid changes with surrounding nucleotide context

nucToAaMuts.description: Amino acid substitutions caused by each nucleotide mutation, keyed by CDS name

alignmentRange.description: Reference coordinate range of the aligned region

aaAlignmentRanges.description: Per-CDS aligned amino acid ranges in reference coordinates

aaUnsequencedRanges.description: Per-CDS unsequenced amino acid ranges (outside the alignment)

pcrPrimerChanges.description: Mutations falling within PCR primer binding regions

totalPcrPrimerChanges.description: Total count of mutations in PCR primer regions

clade.description: Assigned clade label from the nearest reference tree node

privateNucMutations.description: Nucleotide mutations not shared with the nearest reference tree node, subdivided into reversions, labeled, and unlabeled

privateAaMutations.description: Per-CDS amino acid mutations not shared with the nearest reference tree node

cladeFounderInfo.description: Mutations relative to the clade founder node

cladeNodeAttrFounderInfo.description: Per-attribute mutations relative to founder nodes, keyed by attribute name

refNodes.description: Reference node search criteria defined in the dataset

refNodeSearchResults.description: Results of dataset-defined ancestral node searches

relativeNucMutations.description: Nucleotide mutations relative to matched reference nodes of interest

relativeAaMutations.description: Amino acid mutations relative to matched reference nodes of interest

divergence.description: Evolutionary divergence from the root of the reference tree

coverage.description: Fraction of reference positions covered by the query sequence (0.0 to 1.0)

cdsCoverage.description: Per-CDS fraction of amino acid positions covered, keyed by CDS name

qc.description: Quality control results including overall score, status, and per-rule results

customNodeAttributes.description: Clade-like attributes from the nearest tree node, keyed by attribute name

nearestNodeId.description: Internal graph key of the nearest reference tree node

nearestNodeName.description: Name of the nearest reference tree node

nearestNodes.description: Names of equidistant nearest tree nodes when multiple candidates exist

isReverseComplement.description: Whether the sequence was reverse-complemented before analysis

phenotypeValues.description: Computed phenotype scores as defined in the dataset configuration

aaMotifs.description: Amino acid motifs detected in the query sequence, keyed by motif name

aaMotifsChanges.description: Changes in amino acid motifs relative to the reference, keyed by motif name

annotation.description: Genome annotation in query sequence coordinates

NucSub.description: Single-position nucleotide change from reference to query. Display format: `<ref><1-based-pos><qry>`, e.g. `A2147G`.

refNuc.description: Nucleotide in the reference at this position

qryNuc.description: Nucleotide in the query at this position

NucDelRange.description: Contiguous range of nucleotide deletions, grouped from individual `NucDel` entries.

Range_for_Position.description:
Range of positions in a given 1-dimensional coordinate space.

The coordinate space type parameter ensures that positions and ranges in different coordinate spaces have different Rust types and they cannot be used interchangeably.

AaSub.description: Single-position amino acid change in a named CDS. Display format: `<cds>:<ref><1-based-pos><qry>`, e.g. `S:D614G`.

cdsName.description: Name of the coding sequence containing this substitution

refAa.description: Amino acid in the reference at this position

qryAa.description: Amino acid in the query at this position

AaDel.description: Single-position amino acid deletion in a named CDS where the query has a gap at a position present in the reference.

cdsName.description: Name of the coding sequence containing this deletion

refAa.description: Amino acid in the reference at this position

CdsAaRange.description: A set of ranges of amino acid positions in a translated CDS

AaChangesGroup.description: Group of adjacent amino acid changes within a CDS, together with their underlying nucleotide-level changes.

name.description: Name of the coding sequence containing this group

range.description: Codon range spanning all amino acid changes in this group

changes.description: Amino acid changes with nucleotide-level codon context

nucSubs.description: Nucleotide substitutions within the codon ranges of this group

nucDels.description: Nucleotide deletions within the codon ranges of this group

nucDelRanges.description: Nucleotide deletions grouped into contiguous ranges

AaChangeWithContext.description: Amino acid change with nucleotide-level context, including the reference and query codon triplets and their genomic coordinates.

cdsName.description: Name of the coding sequence containing this change

refAa.description: Amino acid in the reference at this position

qryAa.description: Amino acid in the query at this position

nucPos.description: 0-based nucleotide position of the first base of this codon in reference coordinates

refTriplet.description: Reference codon nucleotides (typically 3 bases, more for split codons)

qryTriplet.description: Query codon nucleotides at the same positions

nucRanges.description: Genomic coordinate ranges of the codon bases (multiple ranges for split codons in segmented CDS)

NucDel.description: Single-position nucleotide deletion where the query has a gap at a position present in the reference.

refNuc.description: Nucleotide in the reference at this position

PcrPrimerChange.description: A change in a PCR primer due to mutations

Genotype_for_Nuc.description: A genotype at a single position: the query character without the reference character. Serialized as a compact string like "245A" (1-based position followed by the query character).

qry.description: Query character (nucleotide or amino acid) at this position.

PrivateNucMutations.description: A collection of private nucleotide mutations

privateDeletionRanges.description: All private deletion mutations in form of ranges

reversionSubstitutions.description: A subset of `private_substitutions` which are reversions

labeledSubstitutions.description: A subset of `private_substitutions` which has a label assigned

unlabeledSubstitutions.description: A subset of `private_substitutions` which has no label

NucSubLabeled.description: Nucleotide substitution paired with clade-associated labels from the `mutLabels` map in pathogen.json.

labels.description: Clade or lineage labels associated with this mutation

PrivateAaMutations.description: Private amino acid mutations for one CDS, split into reversions, labeled, and unlabeled categories. Private mutations are differences between the query sequence and its nearest node on the reference tree.

privateSubstitutions.description: All private amino acid substitutions (reversions + non-reversions combined)

privateDeletionRanges.description: Private deletions grouped into contiguous ranges

reversionSubstitutions.description: Substitutions that revert a position back to the reference state

labeledSubstitutions.description: Non-reversion substitutions that match a known clade-specific genotype in the `mutLabels` map

unlabeledSubstitutions.description: Non-reversion substitutions with no matching label

aaChangesGroups.description: Adjacent private mutations grouped into contiguous change blocks with nucleotide context

AaDelRange.description: Contiguous range of amino acid deletions, grouped from individual `AaDel` entries.

range.description: Half-open range of deleted codon positions

AaSubLabeled.description: Amino acid substitution paired with clade-associated labels from the `mutLabels` map in pathogen.json.

labels.description: Clade or lineage labels associated with this mutation

CladeNodeAttrFounderInfo.description: Information about a clade-like node attribute founder

AncestralSearchResult.description: Represents the result of searching for ancestral nodes of interest in the graph

AncestralSearchResultForCriteria.description: Represents the result of searching for ancestral nodes of interest in the graph

AncestralSearchMatch.description: Represents a match found in the ancestral search

RelativeNucMutations.description: Result for a single sequence in the inputs, containing relative nucleotide mutations

RelativeNucMutationsResult.description: Result for a single criterion in the search, containing relative nucleotide mutations

RelativeAaMutations.description: Amino acid mutations relative to a reference ancestor node found by an ancestral search.

search.description: The ancestral node search configuration and match result

result.description: Amino acid mutations relative to the matched ancestor, absent if no ancestor was found

RelativeAaMutationsResult.description: Amino acid mutations computed relative to a matched ancestor node, keyed by CDS name.

criterion.description: The search criterion that matched this ancestor

muts.description: Private amino acid mutations relative to the ancestor, keyed by CDS name

PeptideWarning.description: A warning about a peptide sequence

cdsName.description: Name of the CDS that produced the warning

QcResult.description:
Aggregated quality control results for a single query sequence.

Each individual rule is `None` when disabled in the dataset configuration. The overall score is a quadratic sum of individual rule scores: S = sum(Si^2 / 100).

missingData.description: Result of the missing data (N) rule

mixedSites.description: Result of the mixed (ambiguous) sites (M) rule

privateMutations.description: Result of the private mutations (P) rule

snpClusters.description: Result of the SNP clusters (C) rule

frameShifts.description: Result of the frame shifts (F) rule

stopCodons.description: Result of the premature stop codons (S) rule

overallScore.description: Quadratic aggregate of all individual rule scores

overallStatus.description: Quality category derived from the overall score

QcResultMissingData.description:
Result of the missing data QC rule.

Penalizes sequences with excessive N (missing) characters. Score increases linearly from 0 to 100 as the number of missing sites goes from `scoreBias` to `scoreBias + missingDataThreshold`.

score.description: Numeric QC score for this rule (0-100+)

status.description: Quality category derived from the score

totalMissing.description: Total number of N (missing) characters in the query sequence

missingDataThreshold.description: Effective threshold above which the score reaches 100 (scoreBias + missingDataThreshold from config)

QcStatus.description:
Overall quality category derived from a numeric QC score.

Thresholds: 0-29 = Good, 30-99 = Mediocre, 100+ = Bad.

QcResultMixedSites.description:
Result of the mixed sites QC rule.

Penalizes sequences with ambiguous nucleotides (non-ACGTN, non-gap characters such as R, Y, S). These are often indicative of contamination or co-infection. Score reaches 100 when the count equals `mixedSitesThreshold`.

score.description: Numeric QC score for this rule (0-100+)

status.description: Quality category derived from the score

totalMixedSites.description: Total number of ambiguous (non-ACGTN, non-gap) nucleotide positions

mixedSitesThreshold.description: Threshold from the dataset configuration at which the score reaches 100

QcResultPrivateMutations.description:
Result of the private mutations QC rule.

Private mutations are mutations present in the query sequence but absent from the nearest reference tree node. Excessive private mutations indicate sequencing errors or contamination. Different mutation categories carry different weights. Score reaches 100 when the weighted excess above `typical` equals `cutoff`.

score.description: Numeric QC score for this rule (0-100+)

status.description: Quality category derived from the score

numReversionSubstitutions.description: Number of reversion substitutions (back to reference state, weighted separately)

numLabeledSubstitutions.description: Number of labeled substitutions (known phylogenetically, weighted separately)

numUnlabeledSubstitutions.description: Number of unlabeled substitutions (unknown, weighted at full penalty)

totalDeletionRanges.description: Number of contiguous deletion ranges (adjacent deletions counted as one range)

weightedTotal.description: Weighted sum of all private mutation categories

excess.description: Excess above the typical number of private mutations (weightedTotal - typical)

cutoff.description: Cutoff from the dataset configuration at which the score reaches 100

QcResultSnpClusters.description:
Result of the SNP clusters QC rule.

Detects clusters of private substitutions within a sliding window. If more than `clusterCutOff` substitutions fall within a `windowSize`-nucleotide window, it counts as one cluster. Score equals the number of clusters times `scoreWeight`.

score.description: Numeric QC score for this rule (0-100+)

status.description: Quality category derived from the score

totalSNPs.description: Total number of substitutions across all clusters

ClusteredSnp.description:
A cluster of private nucleotide substitutions within a genomic window.

Clusters indicate localized quality problems, such as contamination or sequencing artifacts in a narrow region of the genome.

start.description: 0-based position of the first substitution in the cluster

end.description: 0-based position of the last substitution in the cluster

numberOfSnps.description: Number of substitutions in this cluster

QcResultFrameShifts.description:
Result of the frame shifts QC rule.

Frame-shifting insertions or deletions disrupt translation and produce garbled proteins or premature stop codons. Known frame shifts listed in `ignoredFrameShifts` in the dataset configuration are excluded from scoring. Score equals the number of non-ignored frame shifts times `scoreWeight`.

score.description: Numeric QC score for this rule (0-100+)

status.description: Quality category derived from the score

frameShifts.description: Frame shifts not in the ignored list (penalized)

frameShiftsIgnored.description: Frame shifts matching the ignored list in dataset configuration (not penalized)

QcResultStopCodons.description:
Result of the premature stop codons QC rule.

Premature stop codons in coding sequences indicate sequencing errors or assembly artifacts, since replicating viruses cannot have stops in essential genes. Known stop codons listed in `ignoredStopCodons` in the dataset configuration are excluded from scoring. Score equals the number of non-ignored premature stops times `scoreWeight`.

score.description: Numeric QC score for this rule (0-100+)

status.description: Quality category derived from the score

stopCodons.description: Premature stop codons not in the ignored list (penalized)

totalStopCodons.description: Number of penalized premature stop codons

stopCodonsIgnored.description: Premature stop codons matching the ignored list in dataset configuration (not penalized)

totalStopCodonsIgnored.description: Number of ignored premature stop codons

StopCodonLocation.description: Location of a stop codon in a CDS

cdsName.description: Name of the coding sequence (CDS) containing the stop codon

codon.description: 0-indexed codon position of the stop codon within the CDS

PhenotypeValue.description: Result for a single phenotype value

name.description: Phenotype name as defined in the dataset configuration

cds.description: CDS used for phenotype score calculation

AaMotif.description: Single regex match of a named amino acid motif in a translated CDS (e.g. glycosylation site, cleavage site).

name.description: Motif type name as defined in `aaMotifs` configuration

cds.description: Name of the coding sequence where this motif was found

position.description: 0-based codon position of the motif match start

AaMotifChanges.description: Diff of amino acid motif matches between reference and query for one named motif type (e.g. glycosylation sites).

preserved.description: Motif matches present in both reference and query

gained.description: Motif matches present in query but absent in reference

lost.description: Motif matches present in reference but absent in query

ambiguous.description: Motif matches present in reference where the query has unresolved amino acids (X)

total.description: Total count of gained plus preserved motifs

AaMotifMutation.description: A motif change record with both reference and query amino acid sequence fragments at the same position.

name.description: Motif type name as defined in `aaMotifs` configuration

cds.description: Name of the coding sequence where this motif occurs

position.description: 0-based codon position of the motif match start

refSeq.description: Amino acid sequence at this position in the reference

qrySeq.description: Amino acid sequence at this position in the query

GeneMap.description: Genome annotation describing all genes and their coding sequences (CDSes) for a pathogen. Parsed from GFF3, Auspice JSON, or internal JSON/YAML format.

genes.description: Genes in the annotation, each containing one or more CDSes.

Gene.description: A gene in the genome annotation, containing one or more coding sequences (CDSes).

index.description: Ordinal index of this gene in the annotation.

id.description: Unique identifier from the GFF3 ID attribute.

name.description: Display name from the GFF3 Name attribute.

range.description: Nucleotide range on the reference sequence spanned by this gene.

cdses.description: Coding sequences contained within this gene.

exceptions.description: GFF3 exception qualifiers (e.g. ribosomal slippage, RNA editing).

attributes.description: Additional GFF3 attributes as key-value pairs.

compatIsCds.description: True when this gene was synthesized from a CDS record that had no parent gene.

color.description: Display color for the genome annotation viewer.

gffSeqid.description: GFF3 seqid column value (reference sequence identifier).

gffSource.description: GFF3 source column value (annotation provenance, e.g. "GenBank").

gffFeatureType.description: GFF3 type column value (feature type, e.g. "gene").

Cds.description: A coding sequence (CDS) that is translated into a protein. May consist of multiple segments (e.g. for programmed ribosomal slippage or spliced genes).

id.description: Unique identifier from the GFF3 ID attribute.

name.description: Display name from the GFF3 Name attribute.

product.description: Protein product name from the GFF3 product attribute.

segments.description: Ordered segments that compose this CDS. Multiple segments represent spliced or slippage features.

proteins.description: Mature protein regions within this CDS (e.g. cleaved polyprotein products).

exceptions.description: GFF3 exception qualifiers (e.g. ribosomal slippage, RNA editing).

attributes.description: Additional GFF3 attributes as key-value pairs, merged from all segments.

compatIsGene.description: True when this CDS was synthesized from a gene record that had no child CDS.

color.description: Display color for the genome annotation viewer.

CdsSegment.description: One contiguous fragment of a coding sequence (CDS). A CDS with programmed ribosomal slippage, splicing, or circular genome wraparound consists of multiple segments.

index.description: Ordinal index of this segment within the parent CDS.

id.description: Unique identifier from the GFF3 ID attribute.

name.description: Display name from the GFF3 Name attribute.

range.description: Nucleotide range on the reference sequence (global coordinates).

rangeLocal.description: Nucleotide range within the concatenated CDS (local coordinates, 0-based).

landmark.description: Circular genome landmark that this segment belongs to, if any.

wrappingPart.description: Position of this segment within a circular genome wraparound.

frame.description: Reading frame (0, 1, or 2) determined by the segment's global start position modulo 3.

phase.description: GFF3 phase (0, 1, or 2): number of bases to skip before the first complete codon.

truncation.description: Whether this segment is truncated at the 5' or 3' end.

exceptions.description: GFF3 exception qualifiers (e.g. ribosomal slippage, RNA editing).

attributes.description: Additional GFF3 attributes as key-value pairs.

compatIsGene.description: True when this segment was synthesized from a gene record that had no child CDS.

color.description: Display color for the genome annotation viewer.

gffSeqid.description: GFF3 seqid column value (reference sequence identifier).

gffSource.description: GFF3 source column value (annotation provenance, e.g. "GenBank").

gffFeatureType.description: GFF3 type column value (feature type, e.g. "CDS").

GeneStrand.description: Strand direction of a genomic feature relative to the reference sequence.

WrappingPart.description:
Marks the position of a CDS segment within a circular genome wraparound.

When a CDS crosses the origin of a circular genome, it is split into linear parts: WrappingStart      : |....<-----| WrappingCentral(1) : |----------| WrappingCentral(2) : |----------| WrappingEnd(3)     : |---->     |

oneOf.description: Segment does not cross the circular genome origin.
First part of a wrapping segment, before the origin crossing.
Intermediate part after wrapping, with its 1-based index within the wrapping group.
Last part of a wrapping segment, with its 1-based index within the wrapping group.

Truncation.description: Whether a CDS segment is truncated (incomplete) and by how many nucleotides.

oneOf.description: No truncation, CDS segment is complete.
Truncated at the 5' end by the given number of nucleotides.
Truncated at the 3' end by the given number of nucleotides.
Truncated at both ends (5' truncation, 3' truncation).

Protein.description: A mature protein region within a CDS (e.g. a cleaved polyprotein product like nsp1, nsp2).

id.description: Unique identifier from the GFF3 ID attribute.

name.description: Display name from the GFF3 Name attribute.

product.description: Protein product name from the GFF3 product attribute.

color.description: Display color for the genome annotation viewer.

ProteinSegment.description: One contiguous fragment of a mature protein region.

id.description: Unique identifier from the GFF3 ID attribute.

name.description: Display name from the GFF3 Name attribute.

range.description: Nucleotide range on the reference sequence.

attributes.description: Additional GFF3 attributes as key-value pairs.

compatIsCds.description: True when synthesized from a CDS record that had no parent gene.

compatIsGene.description: True when synthesized from a gene record that had no child CDS.

color.description: Display color for the genome annotation viewer.

gffSeqid.description: GFF3 seqid column value (reference sequence identifier).

gffSource.description: GFF3 source column value (annotation provenance, e.g. "GenBank").

NextcladeErrorOutputs.title: ResultJsonError
NextcladeErrorOutputs.description: Analysis result for a sequence that failed processing entirely

index.description: Zero-based position of this sequence in the input

seqName.description: Sequence name from the FASTA header

errors.description: Error messages describing why processing failed
